subject: [RFC PATCH 25/45] mm: page_alloc: skip pageblock compatibility threshold in tainted SPBs

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory allocation system to skip a compatibility threshold when trying to relabel a pageblock as non-movable within a tainted superpage block (SPB). The threshold, which is currently enforced by try_to_claim_block(), checks if at least half of the pages in the pageblock are free. However, this check is unnecessary and even harmful within a tainted SPB, where fragmentation has already been accepted. By skipping this check, the patch aims to reduce the number of failed relabeling attempts and prevent the tainting of additional clean SPBs. The change is expected to improve memory allocation performance under heavy load conditions.
